---
title: "Data Analysis"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
author: "L92G04"
format: 
  html: 
    embed-resources: true # Creates a single HTML file as output
    code-fold: true # Code folding; allows you to show/hide code chunks
    code-tools: true # Includes a menu to download the code file 
    # code-tools are particularly important if you use inline R to 
    # improve the reproducibility of your report
table-of-contents: true # (Optional) Creates a table of contents
number-sections: true # (Optional) Puts numbers next to heading/subheadings
execute:
  warning: false
---

## Initial Data Analysis

```{r, results='hide'}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggpubr)
library(janitor)
library(hms)
library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
theme_set(theme_bw())
x = readxl::read_excel("housing-prices-ge19.xls")
```

```{r}
variable_table <- x %>%
  # Get the names of the columns (variables)
  names() %>%
  # Convert the vector of names into a tibble
  tibble::enframe(name = NULL, value = "Variable_Name") %>%
  # Add a second column for the data type (class) of each variable
  dplyr::mutate(
    Data_Type = purrr::map_chr(x, class)
  )
variable_table
```

## Linearity

### Continuous Numeric Variables - Scatter Plots

#### Age, Land Value, Living Area, Lot Size

```{r}
numeric_vars <- x %>% 
  select(Price, Lot.Size, Age, Land.Value, Living.Area)

plot_data_numeric <- numeric_vars %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Price,
    names_to = "Predictor",
    values_to = "Value"
  )

ggplot(plot_data_numeric, aes(x = Value, y = Price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Plot the individual data points
  # Add a linear model trend line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "red") + 
  facet_wrap(~Predictor, scales = "free_x") + # Create a plot for each predictor
  labs(title = "Price vs Continuous Numeric Variables",
       y = "Price") +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Categorical/Ordinal Variables - Box Plots

#### Fireplaces

```{r}
x$Fireplaces <- as.factor(x$Fireplaces)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Fireplaces)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Number of Fireplaces",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Number of Fireplaces")
```

#### Waterfront

```{r}
x$Waterfront <- as.factor(x$Waterfront)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Waterfront)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Waterfront Status",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Waterfront")
```

#### New Construct

```{r}
x$New.Construct <- as.factor(x$New.Construct)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = New.Construct)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by New Construct Status",
       x = "Price",
       y = "New Construct")
```

#### Fuel Type

```{r}
x$Fuel.Type <- as.factor(x$Fuel.Type)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Fuel.Type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Fuel Type",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Fuel Type")
```

#### Heat Type

```{r}
x$Heat.Type <- as.factor(x$Heat.Type)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Heat.Type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Heat Type",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Heat.Type")
```

#### Sewer Type

```{r}
x$Sewer.Type <- as.factor(x$Sewer.Type)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Sewer.Type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Sewer Type",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Sewer Type")
```

#### Rooms

```{r}
x$Rooms <- as.factor(x$Rooms)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Rooms)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Rooms",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Rooms")
```

#### Bathrooms

```{r}
x$Bathrooms <- as.factor(x$Bathrooms)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Bathrooms)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Bathrooms",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Bathrooms")
```

#### Bedrooms

```{r}
x$Bedrooms <- as.factor(x$Bedrooms)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Bedrooms)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Bedrooms",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Bedrooms")
```

#### Central Air

```{r}
x$Central.Air <- as.factor(x$Central.Air)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Central.Air)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Central Air Status",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Central Air Status")
```

### Residual Plots

```{r}
model <- lm(Price ~ Bedrooms_num, data = x)

# Plot residuals vs fitted values
plot(model, which = 1)
```
